logs.title: MBR and EBR ingested — LIS-2024-0441 loaded for review
reasoning: Batch record LIS-2024-0441 received and ingested successfully
Product identified: Lisinopril 10mg Tablets, Product Code LIS-10-TAB
Executing batch record linked to MBR reference MPR-LIS-10-v08 (effective 15-Feb-2024)
Document covers all manufacturing phases: dispensing, granulation, drying, milling, blending, compression, packaging
10-category review initiated across 68 standardised GMP verification checks

logs.title: CRITICAL: EBR records different formulation and product identity — BOM does not match MBR
reasoning: CRITICAL (Header): EBR product name ‘Lisinopril 10mg Film-Coated Tablets’ vs MBR ‘Lisinopril Tablets 10mg (Uncoated tablet, oral)’ — coating status is a critical product attribute; no coating step present in EBR
CRITICAL (BOM): EBR lists Croscarmellose Sodium (Ac-Di-Sol) as disintegrant; MBR requires Crospovidone XL — different materials with different disintegration mechanisms
CRITICAL (BOM): EBR HPMC E5 as binder at 8.00 kg vs MBR Pregelatinised Starch at 15.00 kg; EBR Mannitol 162.52 kg vs MBR 90.00 kg (+80.6%); EBR MCC 44.21 kg vs MBR 120.00 kg (−63.2%)
Equipment PASS: all 5 items (HSG-003, DRY-006, MIL-005, BLN-022, TP-012) calibrated and current on 19–20-Mar-2024
Process CPPs all within spec; all 5 inter-step hold times within validated limits; all rooms EM-compliant

logs.title: MAJOR: 3 consecutive compression IPC supervisor signatures missing (shift handover gap) — Confidence: 52%
reasoning: IPC FAIL (MAJOR): Compression IPC rows 5, 6, 7 (08:30, 08:45, 09:00) have blank Supervisor Initials (Column J) — MBR Section 8.1 and Signatory Matrix require supervisor countersignature at every IPC interval
Gap represents the shift handover window (SM to KA, 08:30–09:00) with no supervisor coverage documented; rows 1–4 signed by SM, rows 8–20 signed by KA
QC PASS (all 7 tests): assay 101.4% LC (spec 90–110%), dissolution 94.2% mean (all 6 vessels ≥80%), LIMS timestamps all within ±1 second — no data integrity flags
Yield and reconciliation PASS: all stage yields in spec; 51,200 labels issued — discrepancy = 0; one minor packaging deviation (PKG-DEV-LIS-2024-031) properly raised and closed
Confidence: 52% — HOLD; QA must investigate whether physical manufacture followed MBR or EBR formulation before disposition can proceed

logs.title: QA escalation drafted — awaiting send approval
reasoning: CRITICAL: EBR formulation is fundamentally different — wrong disintegrant, wrong binder, wrong excipient quantities (not tolerance deviations)
CRITICAL: EBR product description Film-Coated contradicts MBR Uncoated dosage form; no coating step documented
MAJOR: 3 consecutive compression IPC supervisor initials missing at shift handover 08:30–09:00
QA must investigate whether the physical batch was manufactured per MBR or EBR formulation before any disposition decision
All QC results pass — product quality data does not by itself explain the formulation discrepancy

artifacts.label: QA Escalation — BOM Formulation Discrepancy
artifacts.subject: HOLD: Critical BOM Discrepancy — Batch LIS-2024-0441 Lisinopril 10mg
artifacts.body:
Dear QA Head,

Batch LIS-2024-0441 (Lisinopril 10mg Tablets) has been placed on HOLD. The EBR records a formulation that does not correspond to MPR-LIS-10-v08.

CRITICAL FINDING 1 — BOM Formulation Discrepancy:
- Disintegrant: EBR lists Croscarmellose Sodium (Ac-Di-Sol); MBR requires Crospovidone XL
- Binder: EBR lists HPMC E5 at 8.00 kg; MBR specifies Pregelatinised Starch at 15.00 kg
- Mannitol: EBR 162.52 kg vs MBR 90.00 kg (+80.6% deviation)
- MCC: EBR 44.21 kg vs MBR 120.00 kg (-63.2% deviation)

CRITICAL FINDING 2 — Product Identity Discrepancy:
EBR product description: 'Lisinopril 10mg Film-Coated Tablets'. MBR: 'Lisinopril Tablets 10mg (Uncoated tablet, oral)'. No coating step is present in the EBR.

MAJOR FINDING — Missing IPC Signatures:
Compression IPC supervisor initials blank at rows 5-7 (08:30-09:00, shift handover). MBR Section 8.1 requires supervisor countersignature at every IPC check.

Note: All QC results (assay 101.4%, dissolution 94.2%) pass. The formulation discrepancy requires investigation regardless.

Required actions:
1. Determine whether physical manufacture followed MBR or EBR formulation
2. Resolve Film-Coated vs Uncoated product identity discrepancy
3. Obtain retrospective supervisor countersignature or raise formal deviation for IPC gap

Batch remains on HOLD.

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review
Lilly Quality Operations
